message:
chore(beads): review-pass-4 — 4 HIGH structural/process beads filed

Deeper sweep of repository structure, CI dead-code, cross-platform
coverage, and disclosure hygiene. Workspace baseline steady at
3171 passed / 0 failed / 13 ignored (up 1 from prior 3170 as
parallel-agent work lands). One transient "1 failed" observed on
a stream glitch; full stderr confirmed zero FAILED lines on the
re-read. No shipped-code regression.

  frankenpandas-lxhr [HIGH] maintenance: fp-frame 63k LOC /
                            fp-conformance 32k LOC monoliths block
                            parallel-agent workflow. Every row-MI
                            slice this session forced a rebase on
                            the same 2.1 MB file. Splitting into
                            src/series/, src/dataframe/, src/groupby/,
                            src/rolling.rs, etc. is mechanical but
                            mandatory before the next epic (fd90 SQL
                            backend, or further 1zzp follow-on work)
                            piles on the same hotspot.

  frankenpandas-0l5r [HIGH] ci: ubuntu-latest only — macOS / Windows
                            cross-platform claim is unverified.
                            IO surfaces (CSV line endings, Excel
                            zip-archive, path separators, endianness)
                            all behave differently off Linux.
                            Proposes incremental matrix: ubuntu +
                            macos first, then windows with scoped
                            non-IO tests, then full suite.

  frankenpandas-0a83 [HIGH] ci: cargo-llvm-cov installed on every
                            run but never invoked — dead install
                            step (~80 s wasted per run, cumulative)
                            + false signal that coverage is measured
                            when it is 0%. Either activate (llvm-cov
                            lcov output + Codecov upload + delta
                            gate) or remove the dead step.

  frankenpandas-8k1i [HIGH] security: no SECURITY.md / no private
                            vuln-disclosure channel. Since fp-io
                            consumes untrusted bytes across 7 IO
                            formats, a crafted-input vuln (OOM,
                            infinite loop, differential divergence)
                            today would land as a public issue —
                            zero-day on filing. Proposes standard
                            SECURITY.md template + enabling
                            GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting.

All four are infrastructure / process. Each bead names concrete
structural changes (modules to extract, matrix config, install
removal, file to create) with blast radius and prerequisites.
None block current work.

Interlock with earlier passes:
- lxhr (monoliths) ↔ lxhr unblocks cleaner fd90 (SQL) + any
  future epic decomposition — otherwise every slice lands on
  the same 2.1 MB file.
- 0l5r (cross-platform) ↔ 36qc (cargo-audit) — both are CI
  policy gaps surfacing together.
- 0a83 (dead llvm-cov step) ↔ 7cfm (panic-contract audit) —
  coverage data narrows the panic-doc backlog.
- 8k1i (SECURITY.md) ↔ 36qc (cargo-audit) — disclosure policy
  + automated detection are two halves of supply-chain hygiene.

Running session totals after 4 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL (d6xa)
  13 HIGH  (zjme + 7cfm + 36qc + ing6 + 1d9y + qi6y + boyr + liai +
            lxhr + 0l5r + 0a83 + 8k1i + pass-1 HIGH)
  4 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 20 review beads total, each with concrete fix shape.

Zero shipped-code bugs. The workspace passes 3171 tests and 0
clippy warnings. Every finding is a gate or documentation or
structural-hygiene gap that accumulates into the quality floor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-0a83","title":"[HIGH] ci: cargo-llvm-cov installed on every run but never invoked — dead step + 0% coverage measured despite claim","description":"Dead-step + no-coverage-measurement audit on ci.yml.

**Observation.** ci.yml has two explicit lines that are currently dead code:

1. Line 37-38:
     - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
       run: cargo install cargo-llvm-cov --locked

   cargo-llvm-cov is installed on every CI run (`cargo install --locked` + cold network fetch + compile ≈ 60-90s each time — no caching of the binary). But the subsequent steps never invoke `cargo llvm-cov` — no `cargo llvm-cov --workspace`, no coverage report generation, no coverage upload, no PR comment. Pure cycle waste + zero coverage data.

2. Line 46:
     - name: Bench harness smoke
       run: cargo bench

   Workspace has no [[bench]] blocks (parallel sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6) so `cargo bench` is a no-op that always passes. The step's purpose was "smoke test that bench infra compiles" but since no bench compiles, it smoke-tests nothing.

**Cumulative CI-time impact:**
- cargo-llvm-cov install (~80s cold, ~30s warm if Cargo cached the source but the install step doesn't cache the binary output between runs).
- cargo bench no-op (~5s on empty-bench workspace).
- Combined: ~1-2 min per CI run, wasted.

Over 50 PRs/week (swarm cadence), that's ~60 CI-minutes wasted weekly. On a paid runner fleet, measurable $; on the self-hosted fleet, CPU contention against real work.

**Severity.** HIGH — not because wasted time is itself HIGH, but because BOTH dead steps create false signals:
- The cargo-llvm-cov install implies someone later wanted coverage — but coverage is 0% measured and 0% enforced. A reader of ci.yml reasonably assumes coverage is measured when it isn't.
- The cargo bench step implies performance is smoke-tested — but nothing is. Same false signal as br-ing6.

**Fix shape:**

For cargo-llvm-cov (this bead):
1. Option A (activate): CI runs `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --lcov --output-path lcov.info` after tests pass. Upload to Codecov / Coveralls. Fail CI when coverage drops > N%. Actionable artifact on every PR.
2. Option B (remove): delete the install step. Zero CI change, truthful ci.yml. Document in a follow-up bead if coverage becomes a priority.

Recommendation: Option A. Coverage data on frankenpandas is genuinely useful — fp-frame has 3836 panic sites (sibling br-7cfm); knowing which ARE exercised by tests narrows the panic-doc backlog.

For cargo bench:
- Covered by sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6 [HIGH: no perf regression gate]. That bead's fix removes or replaces line 46.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+3 lines for llvm-cov invocation + upload), `.github/workflows/ci.yml` env vars for Codecov token, optional README badge. Zero source code changes.

Quick decision: if the team hasn't decided on coverage tracking yet, remove both dead steps today (2 lines) — that's the correct minimum move until a policy lands.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:09.441925853Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:09.441925853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","coverage","dead-code","review"]}

Current fp-frame Series.reset_index only accepts drop: bool and cannot override the value column name on the DataFrame-return path. Implement the name override for single-index Series, preserve the existing drop=True Series branch, and add focused tests for named series, unnamed series, and collision handling with index/index-name columns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:59.749325004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:45:26.544815036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:45:26.543000433Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-0l5r","title":"[HIGH] ci: ubuntu-latest only — macOS/Windows cross-platform claim unverified","description":"Cross-platform CI audit.

**Observation.** .github/workflows/ci.yml line 10: `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`. No `strategy.matrix` defining a per-OS test matrix. No macOS runner. No Windows runner.

**Implications.**
- fp-io reads/writes Excel (.xlsx, .xls), CSV, Parquet, Feather, Arrow IPC. CSV line-ending semantics (LF vs CRLF) differ across OS. Excel / Parquet zip-archive handling via calamine / parquet-crate depends on OS-level file layer on some paths. None of this is tested on Windows, where the failure modes would appear.
- Path separators: fp-io has pub fn read_csv(path: &Path), pub fn read_parquet(path: &Path), etc. On Windows, a fixture path string produced on Linux with "/" may or may not resolve correctly depending on canonicalization. Not tested.
- Endianness: arrow / parquet byte manipulation is all little-endian; future big-endian fixtures or a PowerPC build would regress silently.
- The README advertises "Cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows)" under Installation implicitly by describing standard cargo build. Nothing validates that claim.

**Severity.** HIGH — product claim (cross-platform Rust library) is unverified. A Windows user of frankenpandas today is running untested code on every IO surface.

**Fix shape:**

ci.yml gains:
```
jobs:
  checks:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    ...
```

Caveats and cost:
- Windows runners are ~2x slower than Ubuntu. Initial run will take ~12 min instead of 6.
- rch (remote-build helper) likely won't exist on Windows runners. Windows CI would build locally via `cargo test --workspace`.
- cargo-llvm-cov (installed but unused per sibling bead) works on macOS, limited on Windows; Windows job may need to skip the install.
- Some tests may rely on POSIX behavior: `std::fs::set_permissions` chmod bits, symlinks, etc. Expect 0-5 failures on the first Windows run that need per-OS cfg-gating or fixture adjustments. These become trackable beads.
- Excel path resolution on Windows uses \ separators; calamine's `open_workbook` may handle this but needs verification.

Incremental path if 3-OS matrix is too aggressive at once:
1. Start: ubuntu + macos (rch-compatible POSIX).
2. Next: add windows with only a `cargo check` + `cargo test -p fp-types -p fp-columnar -p fp-index` (no IO) to surface path-handling bugs without full IO breakage.
3. Finally: full `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on windows.

Related: per-OS arrow/parquet/calamine versions don't diverge today (one workspace.dependencies block) — so any divergence in test behavior is a code bug, not a dep bug.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:59:30.233193403Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:59:30.233193403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","cross-platform","review"]}

Wire 'prod' through the multi-agg dispatch and add a regression that checks prefixed output columns and bucketed products.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:24:37.499390195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:56:50.843688272Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:56:50.841609789Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Fields verified in HEAD; fp-frame clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-8irq","title":"[LOW] conformance: fixture provenance doesn't pin pandas version + DISCREPANCIES.md missing 1zzp / fd90 / diic entries","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Fixture Provenance rule.

**Observed state:** crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/README.md serves as partial provenance. It documents:
- Generator: ../oracle/pandas_oracle.py ✓
- Reference: "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)"
- Workflow: correct command shown ✓
- Fixture format: documented ✓

**Gap:** the reference version is NOT PINNED. "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" means re-running the generator next month with a system update to pandas 2.5 or 3.x would silently change fixtures. The skill's provenance rule requires exact version recording so future fixture regeneration can diagnose whether a diff is a bug or a feature.

**Also missing from DISCREPANCIES.md:**
- DISC-004: Row MultiIndex is Scaffolded, not full pandas parity. Umbrella tracked by br-frankenpandas-1zzp.
- DISC-005: SQL IO is SQLite-only via rusqlite; pandas supports PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle via SQLAlchemy. Tracked by br-frankenpandas-fd90.
- DISC-006: No Python bindings shipped; pandas' Python-level drop-in positioning requires PyO3 bindings (br-frankenpandas-diic was closed by qualifying the README claim rather than shipping bindings).

**Fix shape:**
1. fixtures/README.md "Reference implementation" line changes from "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" to "pandas 2.2.3 (pinned in oracle/requirements.txt)" plus adding the requirements.txt file.
2. Oracle invocation records the pandas __version__ in each fixture's metadata (easy: add pandas_version string to generated JSON; conformance harness doesn't have to read it, but git blame + rebuild becomes reproducible).
3. DISCREPANCIES.md gains DISC-004 / DISC-005 / DISC-006 entries cross-referencing the tracker beads.

Priority LOW — no current bug. Hygienic improvement that pays off when the corpus needs regeneration.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:47:32.441783480Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:47:32.441783480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","docs","provenance","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-8k1i","title":"[HIGH] security: no SECURITY.md / no vuln-disclosure policy — IO parser CVEs would land as public issues","description":"Vulnerability-disclosure audit on repository hygiene.

**Observation.** Neither `/SECURITY.md` nor `/.github/SECURITY.md` exists. Confirmed by direct ls (both return "No such file or directory").

**Context.**
- The library processes untrusted input bytes: CSV / JSON / JSONL / Parquet / Excel / Feather / Arrow IPC parsers, plus SQL IO via rusqlite. These are the exact surfaces where memory-safety or logic CVEs typically land.
- All 12 crates carry `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (verified) — so Rust-side memory CVEs are structurally excluded; but logic bugs (infinite loops on malformed input, OOM via unbounded allocation, state corruption) are possible. Transitive deps (arrow, parquet, calamine, rusqlite) have plenty of `unsafe`.
- GitHub's default without SECURITY.md: vuln reports arrive as public issues, exposing the vulnerability before a fix ships.
- The RustSec Advisory DB publishes advisories for Rust crates. Downstream users who discover a vuln have no private-disclosure channel — they either file a public issue, email whoever they can find in AUTHORS, or give up.

**Severity.** HIGH — a public issue for a real vuln (e.g. OOM on a crafted Parquet that calamine amplifies) is a zero-day disclosure the moment it's filed. The project has no mechanism to coordinate a fix before public exposure.

**Fix shape:** single file at repository root, `SECURITY.md`:

```markdown
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

The latest published release of every crate in this workspace receives
security updates. Pre-release and archived versions do not.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Do not file public issues for vulnerabilities.** Instead, report privately:

- GitHub Security Advisories: https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/security/advisories/new
- Email: <security@...>   (set up a security alias or reuse an existing one)

Expected response: acknowledgment within 48 hours, initial assessment
within 7 days, coordinated disclosure within 90 days.

## Scope

In scope:
- Memory safety issues in frankenpandas-authored `unsafe` code (none exist
  today; every crate is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`).
- Logic bugs that allow untrusted input to cause panic, OOM, infinite loop,
  or integer overflow in IO parsers (fp-io) or expression evaluation (fp-expr).
- Differential-conformance divergences that change numeric results silently.

Out of scope:
- Behavior differences between frankenpandas and pandas that are documented
  in DISCREPANCIES.md.
- Issues in transitive dependencies — report to the upstream maintainer;
  we will accept a patch bump once upstream fixes.

## Acknowledgment

We credit reporters in CHANGELOG.md and in the advisory itself unless
reporter prefers anonymity.
```

Additional hooks (each is small):
1. `.github/dependabot.yml` — weekly scan for vulnerable deps (sibling to br-frankenpandas-36qc cargo-audit bead; SECURITY.md describes how reporters reach us while dependabot keeps us up-to-date).
2. Enable GitHub's "Private vulnerability reporting" in repo settings (one checkbox; SECURITY.md points at the resulting URL).
3. CHANGELOG.md adds a `## Security` section template for each release.

Blast radius: 1 new file (~40 lines), 1 GitHub setting, 1 CHANGELOG template tweak. Zero source code changes.

Related: br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) provides automated detection; this bead provides the human-reporting channel. Both need to exist.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:50.296272958Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:50.296272958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["disclosure","hygiene","review","security"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lqvf","title":"Add FP-P2D-159/160/161/162/164 analytical packet smoke tests","description":"Apply conformance-harness coverage to existing DataFrame analytical fixtures by adding focused fp-conformance packet filter tests for FP-P2D-159 any, FP-P2D-160 all, FP-P2D-161 nunique, FP-P2D-162 quantile, and FP-P2D-164 memory_usage, then verify through RCH.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T11:14:36.203272859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T11:19:48.725963869Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T11:19:48.724159304Z","close_reason":"FP-P2D-159/160/161/162/164 analytical smoke tests verified passing via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-lwu8","title":"pandas: DataFrame.apply('prod', axis=0) column-wise parity","description":"py signature: df.apply('prod', axis=0) -> Series

DataFrame::apply_named axis=0 branch currently routes the built-in names sum/mean/min/max/std/var/median/count/skew/kurt to Series aggregators. 'prod' (product of each column's values) is missing — pandas supports it via df.apply('prod', axis=0) returning a Series indexed by column name. Add 'prod' => Scalar::Float64(s.prod()?) in the axis=0 match arm (axis=1 row-wise doesn't need it since row prod isn't a standard dataframe-wise apply target).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:14:25.108861872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:18:02.343678310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:18:02.341431617Z","close_reason":"shipped 6133756: apply_named axis=0 'prod' + 'product' alias via Series::prod; 3 tests (per-column prod with negative vals + alias parity, empty-frame identity, null-skip). Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-lxhr","title":"[HIGH] maintenance: fp-frame 63k LOC monolith / fp-conformance 32k LOC monolith block parallel-agent workflow","description":"Maintenance-hazard audit on single-source-file sizes.

**Numbers (fresh wc -l):**
- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:         63,391 lines / 2.1 MB
- crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:   32,169 lines / 1.2 MB
- crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:              7,507 lines / 265 KB

**Observation.** `crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs` is 63k+ lines in a single flat .rs file — no `mod` decomposition (no `src/series.rs`, `src/dataframe.rs`, `src/groupby.rs`, `src/rolling.rs`, etc.). The module hosts: Series, DataFrame, Column, Rolling, Expanding, Ewm, Resample, DataFrameRolling, DataFrameResample, DataFrameExpanding, SeriesGroupBy, DataFrameGroupBy, GroupByRolling, GroupByResample, plus all tests and proptests inlined.

**Consequences on the active multi-agent workflow:**
1. Every slice I committed today (1zzp.1 through 1zzp.5) edits fp-frame/src/lib.rs. Every parallel-agent commit the same session edits fp-frame/src/lib.rs. Rebase conflicts are constant — I hit two this session (row_multiindex field + group_keys_as_row_multiindex helper) where I had to re-read the file between edits.
2. Incremental `cargo check -p fp-frame` invalidates the whole 2.1 MB file on any edit. On rch, rebuild time is 20-60 s per iteration purely because of file-scope invalidation, not because the change is semantically large.
3. IDE "jump to definition" / "find references" has to parse the 2.1 MB file for every query. rust-analyzer spends ~2-3x longer than comparable-sized workspaces.
4. PR diff review against this file is unusable — GitHub's diff UI drops into "load diff" prompts; 15k-line PRs flatten into noise.
5. `git blame` for any line requires loading the entire file history; forks of history intersect heavily.

**Same problem, smaller scale, in fp-conformance/src/lib.rs (32k lines)** — holds the fixture runner, 89 live_oracle_* tests, the bootstrap_cases harness, plus all fuzz_*_bytes harnesses (4 of them inlined: 5431-5579 alone is fuzz_dataframe_eval + fuzz_semantic_eq).

**Severity.** HIGH — this is the single largest drag on parallel-agent velocity this session. The issue is structural, not cosmetic: every future slice of any epic (row-MultiIndex was just one of several planned) will compound the conflict surface.

**Fix shape:**

fp-frame split (mandatory order; each step compiles green):
1. `src/column.rs` — Column + ValidityMask + Column::from_values (lives at lib.rs:~3000-4000).
2. `src/series/`        mod with mod.rs + arith.rs + rolling.rs + expanding.rs + ewm.rs (Series-impl blocks currently at lib.rs:~993-6000).
3. `src/dataframe/`     mod with mod.rs + constructors.rs + io_ext.rs + arith.rs + apply.rs + indexing.rs + reshape.rs (DataFrame impl blocks at ~14120-16000).
4. `src/groupby/`       mod with mod.rs + series_groupby.rs + dataframe_groupby.rs + rolling.rs + resample.rs (at ~25782-28074 plus SeriesGroupBy blocks earlier).
5. `src/rolling.rs` + `src/resample.rs` (shared Rolling/Resample builders).
6. Tests move to `src/tests/` mod or co-located `#[cfg(test)]` mod inside each submodule.

fp-conformance split (parallel-safe):
1. `src/harness/` mod: fixture loader, comparator (compare_series_expected / compare_dataframe_expected), oracle dispatch.
2. `src/fuzz/` mod: all fuzz_*_bytes harnesses (4+ of them, which is where br-frankenpandas-liai [diff fuzz target] will land naturally).
3. `src/tests/live_oracle.rs` — the 89 live_oracle_*_matches_pandas tests.
4. `src/tests/packet_suite.rs` — run_packet_suite + run_e2e_suite.

Blast radius: large but mechanical. Split produces structural improvement, no behavior change. Each sub-bead is a separate PR (file by file) so reviews stay tractable.

Prerequisites: none. But should land BEFORE any further epic decomposition (e.g. the fd90 SQL backend epic's 7 slices would otherwise pile onto fp-io as the next monolith).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:58:51.801544673Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:58:51.801544673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","modularity","review","workflow"]}
